title: micropolypodium cornigerum
intro: Micropolypodium cornigerum, also known as the horned polypodium, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Micropolypodium cornigerum include the potential treatment of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its use as a tonic to boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are typically consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
